facts.report_excerpt: = Accessing Report :description: Current owner-oriented maturity report for the Accessing component. :page-slug: accessing-report This page is the current owner-oriented maturity report for *Accessing*. It is written not as a defect log, but as a public-facing readiness portrait: what the component already demonstrates, what maturity band it currently occupies, and which leader-class direction it is converging toward. == Component Portrait Accessing is not shaped as a simple login screen or a lightweight security helper. It reads as a Symfony-oriented account access and account security component: a system intended to manage registration, verification, MFA, password recovery, account locking, and operational discipline around the account lifecycle. The component already shows signs of being treated as a governed subsystem rather than as a convenience layer. Its current shape suggests an internal access and identity surface designed for durability, structured evolution, and integration into a wider ecosystem. == Ecosystem Role Within the broader Smartresponsor direction, Accessing appears to occupy the role of an account access and account security component. Its ecosystem role can be understood across several functions: * as a registration and verification surface, * as an MFA-aware account security layer, * as a password recovery and reset surface, * as a session and lock-discipline component, * and as a governed account-core capability for the ecosystem. This makes Accessing strategically more important than a typical login feature. It points toward a reusable internal access capability that other components can depend on. == Current Maturity Accessing already presents several strong signals of maturity: * a clear account-centric product identity, * explicit Symfony 8 and PHP 8.4 targeting, * strong security package alignment, * multiple test bands, * a governed account model around verification, MFA, lock state, and failure tracking, * and a local quality pipeline for style, static analysis, and tests. In its current public reading, the component is no longer in a prototype-only band. It already behaves like a structured component moving toward release-candidate maturity. == RC Scorecard [cols="2,1,4",options="header"] |=== | Layer | Score to RC | Reading | Product identity | 8.8 / 10 | The component is already clearly framed as an account access application with registration, verification, MFA, recovery, sessions, and security events. | Architecture and domain shape | 8.5 / 10 | The Account-centered model already expresses verification, MFA, locking, failed-login discipline, and account security state as one coherent surface. | Runtime and application surface | 8.4 / 10 | Symfony Security, CSRF, Mailer, Notifier, Rate Limiter, and runtime packages already establish a real access application layer. | QA maturity | 8.1 / 10 | The quality contour is already solid across linting, style, static analysis, tests, and a local full pipeline. | Security posture | 8.9 / 10 | MFA, password recovery, verify-email, rate limiting, lock state, and failed-login tracking form a very strong security signal. | Market-facing alignment | 8.3 / 10 | The component already aligns well with enterprise-class account access and CIAM-style surfaces, though it still reads more as an internal governed component than a public identity product shell. | Overall readiness to RC | 8.5 / 10 | Accessing is already a strong pre-RC component with clear enterprise-class internal access signals. |=== == Leader Line Accessing should not be measured against basic login forms or narrow authentication helpers. Its natural comparison line is closer to the enterprise-class customer identity, account access, and governed authentication category, including the kind of capability bands represented by: * registration and verification, * password recovery and reset, * MFA and second-factor enablement, * session and lock discipline, * and governed operational handling of account security. This means the component is better understood as targeting the same strategic class of problem addressed by stronger account access and customer identity leaders, while expressing that class through a Symfony-native internal component. In that sense, the leader line is not another login page. It is closer to: * enterprise account access class, * enterprise account security class, * enterprise governed identity-surface class, * reinterpreted as an internal ecosystem component. == Strength Profile Several strengths are already visible and worth naming directly. === Account-centric model clarity Accessing already carries a strong center of gravity around the Account entity itself. That matters because many access systems become vague when registration, MFA, verification, recovery, and account security state are spread across disconnected technical surfaces. That fragmentation is not the dominant signal here. The component instead signals a coherent account-centered model. …

key_file_excerpts..github/workflows/gh-pages.yml:
name: gh-pages

on:
  push:
    branches: ["master"]
  workflow_dispatch: {}

permissions:
  contents: write

concurrency:
  group: "docs-gh-pages"
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  deploy:
    if: ${{ github.event_name != 'push' || !contains(github.event.head_commit.message, '[skip docs]') }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout master
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: master
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: "24"

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install build dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y pandoc
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          python -m pip install pyyaml
          npm install

      - name: Build canonical Antora source tree
        run: |
          python3 tools/build_antora_site.py

      - name: Build site with Antora
        run: |
          npx antora antora-playbook.yml --fetch

      - name: Copy public site extras
        run: |
          if [ -f docs/CNAME ]; then
            cp docs/CNAME .site_build/CNAME
          fi
          if [ -d assets ]; then
            mkdir -p .site_build/assets
            cp -R assets/. .site_build/assets/
          fi
          touch .site_build/.nojekyll

      - name: Publish owner-facing site to gh-pages
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"

          workdir="$(mktemp -d)"

          if git ls-remote --exit-code origin gh-pages >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            git fetch origin gh-pages:gh-pages
            git worktree add --force "$workdir" gh-pages
          else
            git worktree add --detach "$workdir"
            (
              cd "$workdir"
              git checkout --orphan gh-pages
            )
          fi

          find "$workdir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name '.git' -exec rm -rf {} +
          cp -R .site_build/. "$workdir"/

          (
            cd "$workdir"
            git add -A
            if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
              git commit -m "docs: publish canonical Antora site"
              git push origin HEAD:gh-pages --force
            else
              echo "No site changes to publish."
            fi
          )

          git worktree remove "$workdir" --force || true
key_file_excerpts..github/workflows/quality-atlas-assessme:
name: quality-atlas-assessment

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      component:
        description: 'Optional component_id to assess (for example: documentating)'
        required: false
        type: string
      mode:
        description: 'Optional run mode override'
        required: false
        type: choice
        options:
          - auto
          - dry-run
          - responses
        default: auto
  schedule:
    - cron: '27 9 */2 * *'

permissions:
  contents: write

concurrency:
  group: quality-atlas-assessment
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  assess:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout master
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: master
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Setup Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v6
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          python -m pip install pyyaml

      - name: Seed current component snapshots
        run: |
          python3 tools/seed_quality_atlas_snapshots.py

      - name: Run scheduled assessment dry-run or AI-backed pass
        id: assessment
        env:
          OPENAI_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENAI_API_KEY }}
          QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL: ${{ vars.QUALITY_ATLAS_OPENAI_MODEL || 'gpt-5' }}
          INPUT_COMPONENT: ${{ github.event.inputs.component || '' }}
          INPUT_MODE: ${{ github.event.inputs.mode || 'auto' }}
        run: |
          mode="dry-run"
          if [ "${INPUT_MODE}" = "responses" ]; then
            mode="responses"
          elif [ "${INPUT_MODE}" = "dry-run" ]; then
            mode="dry-run"
          elif [ -n "${OPENAI_API_KEY}" ]; then
            mode="responses"
          fi

          component_args=""
          selected_component="ALL_ENABLED"
          if [ -n "${INPUT_COMPONENT}" ]; then
            component_args="--component ${INPUT_COMPONENT}"
            selected_component="${INPUT_COMPONENT}"
          fi

          echo "mode=${mode}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "component=${selected_component}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          echo "Assessment mode=${mode} component=${selected_component}"
          python3 tools/run_quality_atlas_assessment.py --mode "$mode" ${component_args}
      - name: Print assessment errors
        if: always()
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          from pathlib import Path
          import json
          root = Path('.sync/quality-atlas/generated/assessment-runs')
          if not root.exists():
              print('No assessment run directory found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          runs = sorted([p for p in root.iterdir() if p.is_dir()])
          if not runs:
              print('No assessment runs found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          run_dir = runs[-1]
          print(f'Latest assessment run: {run_dir}')
          errors = sorted(run_dir.glob('*.error.json'))
          if not errors:
              print('No component error files found.')
              raise SystemExit(0)
          for path in errors:
              print(f'\n===== {path.name} =====')
              try:
                  payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
              except Exception:
                  print(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8', errors='ignore'))
                  continue
              compact = {
                  'component': payload.get('component'),
                  'title': payload.get('title'),
                  'mode': payload.get('mode'),
                  'error_type': payload.get('error_type'),
                  'error': payload.get('error'),
              }
              print(json.dumps(compact, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
          PY

      - name: Write assessment step summary
        if: always()
        run: |
          python3 - <<'PY'
          from pathlib import Path
          import json
          summary_path = Path('.sync/quality-atla

verdict.summary: Refreshed owner-facing snapshot grounded in the repository’s canonical Accessing report and site automation; prior pre-RC scores are retained pending direct upstream code evidence.
strengths: Canonical owner-facing Accessing report is present and maintained in the Antora source tree
Automated docs publishing to gh-pages ensures a reliable public snapshot
Scheduled Quality Atlas assessment workflow is wired for periodic refresh
Repository structure clearly separates canonical content and delivery automation
risks: Current snapshot relies on narrative content rather than direct upstream code evidence
No composer/phpunit or runtime artifacts exist in this repo to verify Accessing claims
Possible drift between the report and the upstream smartresponsor/accessing implementation
next_actions: Ensure the scheduled Quality Atlas assessment ingests upstream facts for component 'accessing' and refreshes this snapshot
Augment the Accessing report with explicit links to upstream repo evidence (e.g., CI badges, composer.json, test configs, security policy) to ground claims
Track report updates alongside upstream releases to minimize drift and keep RC signals current
Add a short evidence block in the report summarizing latest upstream commit refs used for the assessment
notes: This update uses only facts available in this documentation repository and the existing snapshot.
Owner-facing report for Accessing is the primary evidence source for product and positioning.
CI workflows validate site generation and scheduled assessments but do not validate Accessing runtime or QA directly.

product.summary: Component identity and market-facing shape remain materially present per the canonical report; scores held pending upstream code-linked evidence.
risks: Product claims are documented but not cross-linked to upstream release artifacts in this repo
improvements: Embed links in the report to upstream releases and roadmap items to anchor product positioning

architecture.summary: Symfony-oriented architecture shape is described in the report and remains plausible, but this repo lacks direct code artifacts to verify.
risks: Architecture reading is narrative-only without code or design docs in this repo
improvements: Add references in the report to upstream architecture docs and representative code paths

runtime.summary: Runtime surface (security packages, flows) is articulated in the report; verification awaits upstream runtime artifacts.
risks: No runtime configs or package manifests present here to corroborate runtime claims
improvements: Reference upstream environment/config samples and CI status in the report for runtime grounding

qa.summary: Testing and QA posture read as materially present but pre-RC per the report; no local QA artifacts exist to confirm.
risks: Absence of test configs or coverage signals in this repo constrains verification
improvements: Link to upstream test strategy, CI badges, and coverage summaries from the report

ops.summary: Operational and governance posture is narrated in the report; this repo demonstrates site and assessment automation but not Accessing runtime ops.
risks: Operational claims for Accessing are not evidenced by deployment or security artifacts here
improvements: Add pointers in the report to upstream security policy, threat model, and deployment runbooks
Keep the scheduled assessment producing evidence-linked refreshes

market.summary: Business and portfolio alignment signals remain credible via the owner-facing report; evidence remains narrative-first.
risks: Market alignment is not tied to external benchmarks or adoption signals in this repo
improvements: Cite external comparison points and intended customer segments in the report with references

overall.summary: Overall RC direction remains visible from the canonical report, with scores preserved until upstream repository evidence is ingested.
risks: Overall readiness may be overstated if the report drifts from upstream implementation
improvements: Run the scheduled assessment to ingest upstream facts and refresh this snapshot with evidence links
